{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-55438", "cvss": {"score": 5.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-346", "name": "Origin Validation Error"}], "description": "### Summary

Coder's subdomain-based workspace app proxy allowed the same-owner CORS check to be bypassed. When a workspace-name subdomain segment parsed as a UUID, the workspace was resolved by ID without confirming the URL's username matched the real owner, while the CORS middleware trusted the unverified username in the hostname.

> **Note:** Practical exploitation requires subdomain app routing (wildcard hostname) enabled and a victim who visits the attacker's crafted app URL while authenticated.

### Impact

An authenticated user could craft a subdomain encoding their own workspace UUID and a victim's username. If the victim visited the attacker's URL, the attacker's JavaScript could issue credentialed cross-origin `fetch()` requests to the victim's workspace apps and read the responses, exfiltrating data accessible through those apps.

### Patches

The fix validates the subdomain username against the resolved workspace's actual owner and bases the same-owner CORS decision on the authoritative owner identity.

The fix was backported to all supported release lines:

| Release line | Patched version |
|---|---|
| 2.34 | [v2.34.2](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.34.2) |
| 2.33 | [v2.33.8](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.33.8) |
| 2.32 | [v2.32.7](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.32.7) |
| 2.29 (ESR) | [v2.29.17](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.29.17) |

### Workarounds

None.

### Resources

- Fix: #26086, #26085

### Credits

Coder would like to thank Anthropic's Security Team (ANT-2026-22434) for independently disclosing this issue!", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00222, "percentile": 0.12752}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-06T21:14:49Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-55438"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-06T21:14:49Z", "references": ["https://github.com/coder/coder/security/advisories/GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw", "https://github.com/coder/coder/pull/26085", "https://github.com/coder/coder/pull/26086", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/coder/coder/security-advisories/GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/coder/coder", "summary": "Coder's workspace app CORS origin check can be bypassed via UUID-based subdomain spoofing", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-06T21:14:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-5wg6-jmq2-53pw", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.34.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.34.0, < 2.34.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.33.8", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.33.0, < 2.33.8"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.32.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.30.0, < 2.32.7"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.29.17", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.29.17"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Coder's workspace app CORS origin check can be bypassed via UUID-based subdomain spoofing
